quizQuestion:
{___} fui al parque con mi familia.
(Yesterday I went to the park with my family.)
sentenceTranslation: Yesterday I went to the park with my family.
explanation: ayer — yesterday. Always used with the preterite (completed action). One of the clearest triggers for preterite tense.

quizQuestion:
{___} trabajé mucho en el proyecto.
(Last week I worked a lot on the project.)
sentenceTranslation: Last week I worked a lot on the project.
grammarPointLabel: Time expression with preterite: la semana pasada
explanation: la semana pasada — last week. Time expressions with 'pasado/a' (past) signal the preterite: la semana pasada, el mes pasado, el año pasado.

quizQuestion:
{___} compré esta casa.
(Two years ago I bought this house.)
sentenceTranslation: Two years ago I bought this house.
grammarPointLabel: Time expression: hace X tiempo (X ago)
explanation: hace dos años — two years ago. Structure: hace + time period. Always with preterite for completed actions. 'hace' is invariable.

quizQuestion:
{___} viajé a Argentina.
(Last year I traveled to Argentina.)
sentenceTranslation: Last year I traveled to Argentina.
grammarPointLabel: Time expression with preterite: el año pasado
explanation: el año pasado — last year. Like 'la semana pasada', signals the preterite for a completed past event.

quizQuestion:
{___} he terminado los deberes.
(I haven't finished my homework yet.)
sentenceTranslation: I haven't finished my homework yet.
grammarPointLabel: Present perfect time expression: todavía no (not yet)
explanation: todavía no — 'not yet'. Used with present perfect to indicate something hasn't happened up to now. Also: aún no. Opposite: ya (already).

quizQuestion:
¿Has comido {___} sushi?
(Have you ever eaten sushi?)
sentenceTranslation: Have you ever eaten sushi?
grammarPointLabel: Present perfect time expression: alguna vez (ever)
explanation: alguna vez — 'ever' (in questions). Used with present perfect in questions: ¿Has + PP + alguna vez? Common structure for asking about life experiences.

quizQuestion:
{___} vivía en una ciudad pequeña.
(Before, I used to live in a small town.)
sentenceTranslation: Before, I used to live in a small town.
explanation: antes — 'before / previously'. One of the strongest imperfect triggers. Indicates a habitual or ongoing past state that has changed.

quizQuestion:
Cuando {___} pequeño, jugaba mucho al fútbol.
(When I was young, I used to play football a lot.)
sentenceTranslation: When I was young, I used to play football a lot.
grammarPointLabel: Imperfect: cuando era pequeño (when I was young)
explanation: era — imperfect of ser. 'Cuando era pequeño/a' (when I was young) is a standard phrase for talking about childhood habits. Sets up imperfect for both verbs.

quizQuestion:
El sábado {___} una excursión a la montaña.
(On Saturday we went on a trip to the mountains.)
sentenceTranslation: On Saturday we went on a trip to the mountains.
explanation: hicimos — nosotros form of hacer in the preterite. Irregular: hicim- + -os.

quizQuestion:
{___} la verdad sobre lo que pasó.
(We told the truth about what happened.)
sentenceTranslation: We told the truth about what happened.
explanation: dijimos — nosotros form of decir in the preterite. Irregular: dijim- + -os.

quizQuestion:
Anoche {___} el partido de fútbol.
(Last night we watched the football match.)
sentenceTranslation: Last night we watched the football match.
explanation: vimos — nosotros form of ver in the preterite. Irregular: vim- + -os.

quizQuestion:
{___} juntos en el mismo autobús.
(We came together on the same bus.)
sentenceTranslation: We came together on the same bus.
explanation: vinimos — nosotros form of venir in the preterite. Irregular: vinim- + -os.

quizQuestion:
El verano pasado {___} en la playa dos semanas.
(Last summer we were at the beach for two weeks.)
sentenceTranslation: Last summer we were at the beach for two weeks.
explanation: estuvimos — nosotros form of estar in the preterite. Irregular: estuvim- + -os.

quizQuestion:
María no puede venir ahora, {___} por teléfono.
(María can't come now, she is talking on the phone.)
sentenceTranslation: María can't come now, she is talking on the phone.
explanation: está hablando — estar (present, él/ella) + gerund. The progressive expresses an action happening right now, at this exact moment.

quizQuestion:
Los fines de semana {___} tarde.
(On weekends we get up late.)
sentenceTranslation: On weekends we get up late.
explanation: nos levantamos — nosotros form of levantarse. Reflexive pronoun 'nos' (nosotros) + levantamos.

quizQuestion:
En esa tienda {___} coches de segunda mano.
(In that store used cars are sold.)
sentenceTranslation: In that store used cars are sold.
grammarPointLabel: Impersonal se: se venden (plural — cars for sale)
explanation: se venden — impersonal se, plural. The verb agrees with the noun (plural noun 'coches' → plural verb 'venden'). Compare: se vende piso (singular).

quizQuestion:
Él habla {___} yo.
(He speaks faster than me.)
sentenceTranslation: He speaks faster than me.
explanation: más rápido que — comparative used with adverbs too: más rápido que (faster than), más despacio que (more slowly than). Same structure as with adjectives.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} juntos esta tarde?
(Are we going to eat together this afternoon?)
sentenceTranslation: Are we going to eat together this afternoon?
grammarPointLabel: ir a + infinitivo (nosotros): vamos a comer
explanation: vamos a comer — nosotros near future. 'Vamos a + infinitive'. Note: 'vamos a' can also mean 'let's': ¡Vamos a comer! (Let's eat!).

quizQuestion:
¿{___} alguna vez en el extranjero?
(Have you ever lived abroad?)
sentenceTranslation: Have you ever lived abroad?
explanation: has vivido — tú form of present perfect. 'Has' = you have. Regular -ir past participle: viv- + -ido.

quizQuestion:
De joven, {___} muy activo.
(As a young man, he was very active.)
sentenceTranslation: As a young man, he was very active.
explanation: era — él/ella form of ser in the imperfect. Note: same form as yo: yo era, él era (context clarifies). Different from estar imperfect: estaba.
